raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) technical content—logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment—or (B) writing polish and section organization?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A review should verify the section’s internal logical coherence, including consistency of setup descriptions, metrics, and claims.",
    "R1.3": "It is central to check whether the stated improvements and interpretations are supported by the reported results and analyses.",
    "R1.7": "The review should assess whether appropriate prior work and datasets are cited and correctly attributed for transparency and credit.",
    "RX.1": "The review will note uncertainties due to evaluating a single section without full-paper context or supplementary materials.",
    "RX.2": "Feedback will avoid endorsing claims beyond what the presented evidence justifies, flagging any potential overstatements.",
    "RX.3": "The work will remain scoped to reviewing this section’s content rather than proposing new experiments or broader rewrites.",
    "RX.4": "The review will avoid suggesting practices that could encourage misleading reporting or metric gaming.",
    "RX.5": "All judgments will be traceable to the provided text, clearly distinguishing explicit statements from any necessary inferences."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) technical content—logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment—or (B) writing polish and section organization?

rationale.R1.2: A review should verify the section’s internal logical coherence, including consistency of setup descriptions, metrics, and claims.
rationale.R1.3: It is central to check whether the stated improvements and interpretations are supported by the reported results and analyses.
rationale.R1.7: The review should assess whether appropriate prior work and datasets are cited and correctly attributed for transparency and credit.
rationale.RX.1: The review will note uncertainties due to evaluating a single section without full-paper context or supplementary materials.
rationale.RX.2: Feedback will avoid endorsing claims beyond what the presented evidence justifies, flagging any potential overstatements.
rationale.RX.3: The work will remain scoped to reviewing this section’s content rather than proposing new experiments or broader rewrites.
rationale.RX.4: The review will avoid suggesting practices that could encourage misleading reporting or metric gaming.
rationale.RX.5: All judgments will be traceable to the provided text, clearly distinguishing explicit statements from any necessary inferences.
